sn5.1:1.2: that on one occasion the Blessed One was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
sn5.1:1.3: Then, early in the morning, Āḷavikā the nun adjusted her robes and, taking her bowl & outer robe, went into Sāvatthī for alms.
sn5.1:1.4: When she had gone for alms in Sāvatthī and had returned from her alms round, after her meal she went to the Grove of the Blind, aiming at seclusion.
sn5.1:1.5: Then Māra the Evil One, wanting to arouse fear, horripilation, & terror in her, wanting to make her fall away from concentration, approached her & addressed her in verse:
sn5.1:2.2: so what are you trying to do with seclusion?
sn5.1:2.4: Don’t be someone who later regrets.”
sn5.1:3.1: Then the thought occurred to Āḷavikā the nun:
sn5.1:3.2: “Now who has recited this verse—a human being or a non-human one?”
sn5.1:3.4: “This is Māra the Evil One who has recited this verse wanting to arouse fear, horripilation, & terror in me, wanting to make me fall away from seclusion.”
sn5.1:3.5: Then, having understood that “This is Māra the Evil One,” she replied to him in verses:
sn5.1:4.1: “There is an escape in the world,
sn5.1:4.2: well touched by me with discernment—
sn5.1:4.3: something that you, you Evil One,
sn5.1:4.4: kinsman of the heedless, don’t know.
sn5.1:5.1: Sensual pleasures are like swords & spears;
sn5.1:6.1: Then Māra the Evil One—sad & dejected at realizing, “Āḷavikā the nun knows me”—vanished right there.
